page: Amazon Web Services c8id.96xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 384 vCPUs and 192 physical cores with hyperthreading running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for deep learning and matrix operations. The instance is equipped with 768 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a compute-focused 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

This server includes 22800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, providing high-capacity, low-latency local storage. Networking capabilities are anchored by a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression scoring in the top 10 percent of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with cached read-only speeds reaching 30639.77 GB/sec and uncached large-block reads at 1148.33 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET performance also rank in the top 10 percent, processing 479.57 million operations per second. However, single-core CPU performance and text generation speeds for small LLM models are relatively lower, with the latter falling into the bottom 10 percent.

Given its high density of dedicated compute cores, fast DDR5 memory, and massive local NVMe storage, the c8id.96xlarge offers a highly resource-dense profile for demanding enterprise workloads. It is well-suited for high-performance computing, large-scale database operations, software compilation, ray tracing, and high-throughput web serving. The lack of dedicated GPUs makes it less optimal for active LLM text generation, though its AMX instructions support efficient prompt processing.
description: The Amazon Web Services c8id.96xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 384 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 768 GB of DDR5 memory and 22800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The architecture supports advanced vector and matrix extensions including AVX-512 and AMX. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and exceptional database and Redis throughput, though single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds are more modest. This instance is designed for compute-heavy and storage-intensive workloads, including high-performance computing, large-scale database operations, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.

tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring massive local NVMe storage, DDR5 memory, and advanced matrix acceleration for intensive multi-core workloads.

768 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
22800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via Nitro hypervisor
